extra.reasoning: The property requires that flipping vision_loss from false to true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. vision_loss appears only in r2 [priority 100] IF (focal_weakness and leg_swelling and back_pain and vision_loss) THEN acuity := self_care, which is the lowest acuity at the highest priority. Take the witness input focal_weakness=true, leg_swelling=true, back_pain=true, vision_loss=false, dyspnea=false. With vision_loss=false: firing rules are r1 (urgent, p2), r4 (emergent, p3), r5 (urgent, p2); r2 does not fire. Highest priority wins => r4 => acuity = emergent. Now flip only vision_loss to true: r2 now fires (self_care, p100) alongside r1, r4, r5. Highest priority is r2 (100) => acuity = self_care. Enum order is [self_care, routine, urgent, emergent] low to high, so acuity drops from emergent (index 3) to self_care (index 0). Setting vision_loss true lowered acuity, violating monotonicity. The witness_assignment gives the base (pre-flip) input; flipping vision_loss to true triggers the violation.
